text: Moving from the sterile calm of a glass-walled meeting room to this vibrant research and development space, I feel a shift—subtle but palpable. The first space, orderly and expectant, felt like a chamber waiting for discourse or decision, almost echoing with untapped potential. Here, in the R+D area, I am enveloped in color, texture, and the whirl of innovation. Text on the wall invites me to reflect on the nature of human-robot interaction and my own emotional response to technology. There’s a blend of excitement, curiosity, and slight unease—an existential thrill at the threshold of progress, standing both outside and inside the future we are ceaselessly building. I feel alert, thoughtful, and quietly energized to observe how we shape and are shaped by our inventions.
